---
title: "Receptor Scan Metric Comparison"
---

# Summary
Compares two receptor scan metrics across all receptor pairs for a chosen target cell
type. Each receptor pair is plotted as a point with x = metric from scan 1 and
y = metric from scan 2. Useful for comparing selectivity vs. EMD/KL divergence,
two binding model scans with different valencies, or any other pair of scan outputs.

# Inputs
- Two CSV files produced by `run_selectivity_scan` or `run_KL_EMD_scan`
  (may be the same file to compare two metrics within it)
- `SCAN_PATH_1`, `SCAN_PATH_2`: paths to those CSVs
- `METRIC_1`, `METRIC_2`: column names to extract from each scan
  - Selectivity scans: `"Selectivity"`, `"Kx_star"`, `"Affinity_Receptor_N"`
  - Distribution scans: `"EMD"`, `"KL_Divergence"`
- `AXIS_1_LABEL`, `AXIS_2_LABEL`: human-readable names for each axis
- `TARG_CELL_TYPE`: target cell type to extract from both scans

# Outputs
- Scatter plot: x = metric 1, y = metric 2
  - Gray: neither metric is high
  - Blue: high on metric 1 only
  - Orange: high on metric 2 only
  - Red: high on both; point size scales with Euclidean distance from the origin
    (computed on z-score-normalized axes), and a reference arc marks the labeling cutoff
  - Gold stars (★): global top-N pairs by individual-axis metric
  - Purple diamonds (◆): user-specified pairs of interest, always labeled

```{python}
%config InlineBackend.figure_formats = ['svg']
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patheffects as pe
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# ── Parameters ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SCAN_PATH_1 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260212_full_geom_sweep.csv"
SCAN_PATH_2 = "/home/sama/receptor_sweep_data/20260319_dist_scan.csv"

# Column to extract from each scan CSV.
# Selectivity scans: "Selectivity", "Kx_star", "Affinity_Receptor_0", etc.
# Distribution scans: "EMD", "KL_Divergence"
METRIC_1 = "Selectivity"
METRIC_2 = "KL_Divergence"

AXIS_1_LABEL = "Binding model selectivity (valency [2, 1, 1])"
AXIS_2_LABEL = "KL Divergence"

TARG_CELL_TYPE = "Treg"

# Percentile threshold (0–100) above which a metric value is considered "high"
HIGH_PERCENTILE = 90

# Number of auto-labeled points per category (high_1, high_2, both)
N_LABEL = 5

# Number of globally top-ranked pairs to highlight with a star per axis
N_TOP_OVERALL = 1

# User-specified receptor pairs to always label (names must match scan data exactly).
# Each entry is a (rec1, rec2) tuple; order does not matter.
HIGHLIGHT_PAIRS: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
    # ("CD26-2", "CD26-2"),
    # ("Integrin-7", "CD4-1"),
    # ("CD4-1", "CD45RO"),
]

# ── Load scans ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def load_scan_flat(path, metric_col, cell_type):
    """Return DataFrame with columns rec1, rec2, metric for the given cell type."""
    raw = pd.read_csv(path)
    assert cell_type in raw["Cell_Type"].values, f"{cell_type!r} not found in {path}"
    assert metric_col in raw.columns, f"{metric_col!r} not a column in {path}"
    sub = raw[raw["Cell_Type"] == cell_type][["Receptor_1", "Receptor_2", metric_col]].copy()
    return sub.rename(columns={"Receptor_1": "rec1", "Receptor_2": "rec2", metric_col: "metric"}).reset_index(drop=True)

scan1 = load_scan_flat(SCAN_PATH_1, METRIC_1, TARG_CELL_TYPE)
scan2 = load_scan_flat(SCAN_PATH_2, METRIC_2, TARG_CELL_TYPE)

# ── Align receptor sets ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df = (
    pd.merge(scan1, scan2, on=["rec1", "rec2"], suffixes=("_1", "_2"))
    .rename(columns={"metric_1": "s1", "metric_2": "s2"})
    .dropna(subset=["s1", "s2"])
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
assert len(df) > 0, "No valid (non-NaN) receptor pairs found in both scans"

# ── Categorise points ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
thresh1 = np.nanpercentile(df["s1"], HIGH_PERCENTILE)
thresh2 = np.nanpercentile(df["s2"], HIGH_PERCENTILE)

high1 = df["s1"] >= thresh1
high2 = df["s2"] >= thresh2

df["category"] = "neither"
df.loc[high1 & ~high2, "category"] = "high_1"
df.loc[~high1 & high2, "category"] = "high_2"
df.loc[high1 & high2,  "category"] = "both"

# Euclidean distance on z-score-normalized axes so both metrics contribute equally
s1_z = (df["s1"] - df["s1"].mean()) / (df["s1"].std() + 1e-9)
s2_z = (df["s2"] - df["s2"].mean()) / (df["s2"].std() + 1e-9)
df["r"] = np.sqrt(s1_z**2 + s2_z**2)

# ── Select auto-labeled points ────────────────────────────────────────────────
def top_n_by(sub_df, sort_col, n):
    return sub_df.nlargest(n, sort_col)

auto_label_rows = pd.concat([
    top_n_by(df[df["category"] == "high_1"], "s1", N_LABEL),
    top_n_by(df[df["category"] == "high_2"], "s2", N_LABEL),
    top_n_by(df[df["category"] == "both"],   "r",  N_LABEL),
]).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])

# ── Global top-N pairs per axis (starred) ─────────────────────────────────────
top_overall_rows = pd.concat([
    df.nlargest(N_TOP_OVERALL, "s1"),
    df.nlargest(N_TOP_OVERALL, "s2"),
]).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])

# ── User-specified pairs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
user_rows_list = []
for req_rec1, req_rec2 in HIGHLIGHT_PAIRS:
    match = df[
        ((df["rec1"] == req_rec1) & (df["rec2"] == req_rec2))
        | ((df["rec1"] == req_rec2) & (df["rec2"] == req_rec1))
    ]
    if match.empty:
        print(f"Warning: requested pair ({req_rec1}, {req_rec2}) not found in data.")
    else:
        user_rows_list.append(match.iloc[[0]])
user_rows = (
    pd.concat(user_rows_list).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])
    if user_rows_list
    else pd.DataFrame(columns=df.columns)
)

# ── Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 7))

for cat, style in {
    "neither": dict(color="lightgray", zorder=1, alpha=0.6, s=18, label="Neither"),
    "high_1":  dict(color="#3A86FF",   zorder=3, alpha=0.85, s=40,
                    label=f"High {AXIS_1_LABEL} only"),
    "high_2":  dict(color="#FF9F1C",   zorder=3, alpha=0.85, s=40,
                    label=f"High {AXIS_2_LABEL} only"),
}.items():
    sub = df[df["category"] == cat]
    ax.scatter(sub["s1"], sub["s2"], **style)

both_sub = df[df["category"] == "both"].copy()
if not both_sub.empty:
    r_min, r_max = both_sub["r"].min(), both_sub["r"].max()
    sizes_both = (
        40 + 200 * (both_sub["r"] - r_min) / (r_max - r_min)
        if r_max > r_min
        else np.full(len(both_sub), 80)
    )
    ax.scatter(
        both_sub["s1"], both_sub["s2"],
        s=sizes_both, color="#E63946", zorder=4, alpha=0.95,
        label="High on both (size ∝ Euclidean dist.)",
    )
    labeled_both = both_sub.nlargest(N_LABEL, "r")
    if len(labeled_both) == N_LABEL:
        r_ref_z = labeled_both["r"].min()
        s1_std, s2_std = df["s1"].std() + 1e-9, df["s2"].std() + 1e-9
        theta_arc = np.linspace(0, np.pi / 2, 200)
        arc_x = df["s1"].mean() + r_ref_z * s1_std * np.cos(theta_arc)
        arc_y = df["s2"].mean() + r_ref_z * s2_std * np.sin(theta_arc)
        x_mask = (arc_x >= ax.get_xlim()[0]) & (arc_y >= ax.get_ylim()[0])
        ax.plot(
            arc_x[x_mask], arc_y[x_mask],
            color="#E63946", linewidth=1.2, linestyle=":", alpha=0.55,
            zorder=3, label=f"Both: top-{N_LABEL} Euclidean cutoff",
        )

if not user_rows.empty:
    ax.scatter(
        user_rows["s1"], user_rows["s2"],
        marker="D", s=80, color="#9B5DE5", zorder=6, alpha=1.0,
        label="User-specified pairs",
    )

ax.scatter(
    top_overall_rows["s1"], top_overall_rows["s2"],
    marker="*", s=220, color="#FFD700", edgecolors="#888", linewidths=0.5,
    zorder=7, alpha=1.0, label=f"Top-{N_TOP_OVERALL} per axis",
)

ax.axvline(thresh1, color="steelblue", linewidth=1.0, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
ax.axhline(thresh2, color="darkorange", linewidth=1.0, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)

all_label_rows = pd.concat([auto_label_rows, top_overall_rows, user_rows]).drop_duplicates(subset=["rec1", "rec2"])
for _, row in all_label_rows.iterrows():
    label = f"({row['rec1']}, {row['rec2']})"
    txt = ax.text(row["s1"], row["s2"], label, fontsize=7, ha="left", va="bottom")
    txt.set_path_effects([pe.withStroke(linewidth=2, foreground="white")])

ax.set_xlabel(AXIS_1_LABEL, fontsize=13)
ax.set_ylabel(AXIS_2_LABEL, fontsize=13)
ax.set_title(
    f"{AXIS_1_LABEL} vs. {AXIS_2_LABEL}\nTarget: {TARG_CELL_TYPE}  "
    f"(threshold = {HIGH_PERCENTILE}th percentile)",
    fontsize=12, pad=12,
)
ax.tick_params(labelsize=11)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# Tabular summary of all annotated receptor pairs
summary = all_label_rows[["rec1", "rec2", "s1", "s2", "r", "category"]].copy()
summary.columns = ["Receptor 1", "Receptor 2", AXIS_1_LABEL, AXIS_2_LABEL, "Euclidean dist. (norm.)", "Category"]
summary = summary.sort_values("Category").reset_index(drop=True)
summary[[AXIS_1_LABEL, AXIS_2_LABEL, "Euclidean dist. (norm.)"]] = summary[
    [AXIS_1_LABEL, AXIS_2_LABEL, "Euclidean dist. (norm.)"]
].round(4)
print(summary.to_string(index=False))
```
